rulings.Case Title: MULTICELL IMMUNOTHERAPEUTICS, INC. A DELAWARE VS. GT BIOPHARMA, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 29, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER MULTICELL IMMUNOTHERAPEUTICS, INC.'S Notice Of Motion On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Petitioner Multicell Immunotherapeutics, Inc.'s petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. Respondents failed to file a timely petition to vacate the arbitration award or a response requesting vacation of the award within the mandatory 100 day period. (CCP 1288; 1288.2; Douglass v. Serenivision, Inc. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 376, 384.) "[B]ecause defendants did not bring a timely petition or response to correct or vacate the award, the trial court had no choice but to disregard defendants' challenge and 'confirm the award as made.' (§ 1286.)" (Eternity Investments, Inc. v. Brown (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 739, 742.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Multicell is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which sets forth the relief granted in the arbitration award and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KATY CHMURA VS. LAWRENCE CHOY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2018, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT JAKE NG, STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Response To Subpoenas. Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Prior to the hearing, the parties are instructed to meet and confer regarding a suitable stipulated protective order to protect documents produced pursuant to the following order. Cross- Defendants Jake Ng and State Farm General Insurance Company's Motion to Motion to Compel Wells Fargo N.A. and Fremont Bank to Respond to Subpoenas is granted in part. The subpoenas are overbroad. Therefore Wells Fargo N.A. is ordered to produce, on or before June 8, 2018, only records of insurance documents and proof of insurance that were submitted by Lawrence Choy, loan reference number 0081413569, in connection with loan applications for the property located at 2433 Santiago Street, San Francisco, CA. Any Social Security Numbers and/or Taxpayer ID numbers shall be redacted. Fremont Bank is ordered to produce, on or before June 8, 2018, only records of insurance documents and proof of insurance that were submitted by Lawrence Choy, loan reference number 8000055379, in connection with loan applications for the property located at 2311 32nd Avenue, San Francisco, CA. Any Social Security Numbers and/or Taxpayer ID numbers shall be redacted. The motion is denied as to all other documents sought in the subpoenas, which constitute personal financial records protected by the right to privacy. Cross-Defendants have not shown how the other personal financial records sought by the subpoenas are directly relevant to Cross-Defendants' defense. "The proponent of discovery of constitutionally protected material has the burden of making a threshold showing that the evidence sought is 'directly relevant' to the claim or defense." (Harris v. Superior Court (1992) Cal.App.4th 661, 665.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WHITNEY R. LEEMAN VS. STARBUCKS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlements And Consent Judgments As To Defendants R.C. Bigelow, Inc.; Teance Fine Teas; The Hain Celestial Group, Inc.; And Walong Marketing, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 29, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF WHITNEY LEEMAN'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlements And Consent Judgments As To Defendants R.C. Bigelow, Inc.; Teance Fine Teas; The Hain Celestial Group, Inc.; And Walong Marketing, Inc. Hearing required. At the hearing the court will ask the parties and the Attorney General to address two portions of the AG's objection: 1) "Leeman is ¿ now bound by that standard." (5:13) and 2) "Whatever merit her claims might have had when she first filed the case and argued for a lower standard, that changed when she agreed to the 10 ppb standard, thereby admitting that she no longer has claims against companies that meet this standard. The Court should dismiss Bigelow, Celestial, and Walong." (11:13-16). Please be prepared to address whether there is any authority for or against the AG's positions that plaintiff Whitney Leeman is "bound" by the 10ppb standard and this court is empowered to dismiss Bigelow, Celestial, and Walong.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS MARSONER ET AL VS. FCA US LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents By Fca Us Llcs Dealership Personnel Memorandum In Support Of Motion And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of 2,422.50
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF THOMAS MARSONER, ANNABELLE DARAUYA's Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents By Fca Us Llcs Dealership Personnel Memorandum In Support Of Motion And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of 2,422.50. Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties should come prepared to confirm a schedule for depositions of dealership personnel to go forward within twenty days and to address plaintiffs' request for sanctions. Defendants should be prepared to substantiate their argument that the dealership service personnel would incur a significant financial burden if they appear for deposition one mile from the dealership. Plaintiffs' Motion to Compel Deposition Attendance and Production of Documents by FCA US LLC's Dealership Personnel and Request for Sanctions in the Amount of $2,422.50 is granted in part. Defendants are ordered to produce, within twenty days, all documents responsive to the Requests for Production set forth in the notices of deposition for FCA Service Advisors Ray Cooper, Dwayne White, Paul Galvao, and FCA Technician Nos. 41, 57 and 383. If any documents are withheld based on a claim of privilege, defendants must provide a privilege log. As to the depositions of FCA's Person Most Qualified to Testify and Custodian of Records and related document production, that part of the motion is CONTINUED until June 22, 2018 in light of plaintiffs' recently filed Motion to Compel Deposition Attendance and Production of Documents by FCA US LLCS Dealership Personnel and Request for Sanctions in the Amount of $2,197.50, which specifically addresses depositions of FCA's Person Most Qualified to Testify and Custodian of Records, and is set for hearing on June 22, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ilana Diamond'S Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of Commission Authorizing Out Of State Deposition In Accordance With Code Of Civil Procedure Section 2026.010
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2018, Line 4, Plaintiff Ilana Diamond's Motion For Issuance Of Commission Authorizing Out Of State Deposition In Accordance With Code Of Civil Procedure Section 2026.010. Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Judge Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling. The parties are ordered to appear. Counsel should be prepared to discuss (1) whether or not the topic of communication between the plaintiff and Ms. Bushaw is governed by the attorney client or attorney work-product privilege; (2) defendant's Twelfth Affirmative Defense and defendant's alleged independent investigation of plaintiff's claims; (3) the applicability of Wellpoint Health Networks, Inc. v. Superior Court (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 110, D.I. Chadbourne, Inc. v. Superior Court (1964) 60 Cal.2d 723, and Kaiser Found. Hospitals v. Superior Court (1998) 66 Cal.App. 4th 1217; and (6) the documents at issue, including the investigation report. Defense counsel is instructed to bring its retention agreement with Perkins Coie to the hearing for an in camera review, as offered in defendant's motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN CLOPTON VS. CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 29, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION, MICHAEL PICKER, CARLA PETERMAN, LIANE RANDOLPH, MARTHA ACEVES, CLIFFORD RECHTSCHAFFEN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants California Public Utilities Commission, Michael Picker, Carla Peterman, Liane Randolph, Martha Aceves, and Clifford Rechtschaffen's demurrer to the first and third causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Karen Clopton for retaliation in violation of the Whistleblower Protection Act and race discrimination in violation of Government Code 12940 is sustained as to the first cause of action alleged against Ms. Aceves, Ms. Peterman and Mr. Rechtschaffen with twenty days leave to amend and overruled as to all other grounds asserted in the demurrer. While she did so as to Mr. Picker and Ms. Randolph, Ms. Clopton has not sufficiently alleged facts showing that Ms. Aceves, Ms. Peterman and Mr. Rechtschaffen committed a retaliatory act because of a protected disclosure she made. The race discrimination claim is adequately alleged by Ms. Clopton. She alleged that she is a member of a protected class; she performed competently in the position she held; she suffered adverse employment actions culminating in her termination; there are circumstances suggesting discriminatory motive (the hiring of a trainer over the complaints of Ms. Clopton that he made racists remarks and the statement "Are you ready to meet with three white men with white hair?"). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Clopton is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES VARGA VS. TWITCH INTERACTIVE, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 29, 2018, Line 14, PLAINTIFF JAMES VARGA'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Plaintiff James Varga's application for the pro hac vice admission of Eric Schmitt is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)
